Do you see any round bowl-shaped basket with a dark handle?  [03:11:49] director: If you’re not seeing the band, prioritize these cues for basket 2: very deep round bowl/hemisphere body + a single thick, very dark arched handle attached at the sides. [03:13:48] director: For basket 5, ignore color and look for structure: it’s the only one that’s a shallow rectangle with straight-ish sides AND a thick U-shaped handle that looks like a twisted braid/rope. It’s more “cat-like” than the mouse in basket 1. Confirm when set.  [03:14:29] director: Basket 7: a rectangular basket with a high arched handle and a two-tone look—greenish/teal vertical slats on the body, with a warm orange-brown rim and handle braid. [03:14:39] director: Basket 8: a small, squat oval basket with one handle; overall gray/brown tone with distinct horizontal light-and-dark striping in the weave, and a darker gray handle. [03:16:00] director: Basket 9: a tall cylindrical wicker basket (like a standing hamper) with two small loop handles near the top rim (one on each side). Let me know when you’ve found it.  [03:09:34] director: Basket 2: a deep, round bucket-style wicker basket with one thick, dark-brown arched handle; the body is very bowl-like/hemispherical and there’s a lighter tan band around the top rim. Confirm when you’ve got it.  [03:10:48] director: Yes—basket 2 has a lighter tan band/stripe running around near the top rim (just below the handle). The handle itself is dark brown.  [03:11:14] director: For basket 2, look for the ONLY one that’s a nearly perfect half-sphere “bucket” with a single high arch handle in very dark wood. The weave is fine and uniform, and there’s a pale strip/band around the upper rim area. It’s not shallow like a tray and not rectangular—more like a rounded cauldron. Do you see any round bowl-shaped basket with a dark handle?  [03:11:49] director: If you’re not seeing the band, prioritize these cues for basket 2: very deep round bowl/hemisphere body + a single thick, very dark arched handle attached at the sides. The body is medium-brown wicker with fine weaving; rim is slightly lighter than the body but the “band” may be subtle. Please place that deep round one with the dark handle as position 2, and tell me when set.  [03:11:59] director: Basket 3: a rectangular picnic-style wicker basket with a tall vertical handle in the center, and its lid is open/hinged up to the RIGHT side (so you can see the interior). Not an open-top basket—has that flap lid. Confirm when placed.  [03:12:10] director: Basket 4: an oval, light-colored basket with a single arched handle; there are colored wrap accents on the handle (little red/green bands), and the basket has a slightly pointed/teardrop shape with the opening angled—looks a bit like it’s “leaning.” Let me know when you’ve got it.  [03:13:15] director: Basket 5: a low, rectangular basket (bread-basket shape) with a single rounded handle; the handle is made of twisted rope-like wicker, and the basket’s sides have a chunky, blocky weave pattern. Confirm when placed.  [03:13:48] director: For basket 5, ignore color and look for structure: it’s the only one that’s a shallow rectangle with straight-ish sides AND a thick U-shaped handle that looks like a twisted braid/rope. [03:14:19] director: Basket 6: a lidded novelty basket shaped like a cat face viewed from above—round, pale straw, with two small ears, painted eyes/whiskers, and it sits on little feet. It’s more “cat-like” than the mouse in basket 1. Confirm when set.  [03:14:29] director: Basket 7: a rectangular basket with a high arched handle and a two-tone look—greenish/teal vertical slats on the body, with a warm orange-brown rim and handle braid. The top edge has a braided border. Let me know when you find it.  [03:14:39] director: Basket 8: a small, squat oval basket with one handle; overall gray/brown tone with distinct horizontal light-and-dark striping in the weave, and a darker gray handle. It looks “banded”/striped across the body. Confirm when placed.  [03:15:33] director: Let me clarify basket 8: it’s short and wide (not tall), like a little bucket/oval tub with a single arch handle. The weave alternates dark gray-brown and lighter tan bands around the body, giving it a striped look. It’s smaller than the tall cylindrical basket (basket 9) and not rectangular like basket 7. Do you see an oval basket with obvious horizontal striping?  [03:16:00] director: Basket 9: a tall cylindrical wicker basket (like a standing hamper) with two small loop handles near the top rim (one on each side). No big arching handle—just those two little side loops.  [03:16:24] director: Basket 10: a shallow, round tray-like basket with a single arch handle; the base has a noticeable spiral coil pattern, and the handle is light-colored. It’s the flattest, most “plate-like” round one. Confirm when placed.  [03:16:40] director: Basket 11: a large, open-top round basket with two short ear-like handles on the sides (not a tall arch). The weave is dense and diagonally patterned, and the rim flares slightly outward. It looks like a chunky storage bin. Confirm when set.  [03:16:51] director: Basket 12: a medium open-top basket with a single arched handle and a distinctive wavy/scalloped rim opening (dips in the middle). Light brown wicker, more rounded-square than oval, and the handle attaches at the high points of that wavy rim. Confirm when placed.  [15:24:19] director: Great—now basket 2: pick the deep, round “half-sphere” bucket basket with ONE thick, very dark brown arched handle. [15:24:55] director: Basket 7 is the rectangular tote with a high arch handle and a very distinctive two‑tone look: the body has green/teal vertical striping, while the rim and handle are warm orange‑brown with a braided top border. [15:25:01] director: Basket 3: the rectangular picnic-style wicker basket with a tall vertical handle in the center, and a hinged lid that’s visibly open/flipped up to the RIGHT side (you can see inside). Please confirm when you’ve placed basket 3.  [15:25:42] director: Basket 4: an oval, light-straw basket with a single arched handle that has little colored wrap bands (red/green) near where the handle meets the sides. The opening is slightly asymmetrical/teardrop like it’s leaning. Confirm when you’ve placed basket 4.  [15:27:09] director: Basket 12: open-top light-brown wicker with ONE arched handle, but the key is the rim is wavy/scalloped—there’s a noticeable dip in the middle of the opening (not a straight rectangle). The body is kind of rounded-square. Confirm when you’ve got that one.  [15:27:16] director: Confirmed baskets 4–11. Now double-check basket 1: it’s the mouse/rat-shaped wicker basket in side view with a pointed snout facing right and a flat slot/strap opening on top (not the round cat face). Do you have that placed as position 1?  [15:27:34] director: Let me clarify basket 4: it’s a LIGHT straw-colored OVAL basket with ONE arch handle, but the giveaway is the handle has small COLORED wrap bands (little red/green bits) where the handle meets the sides. The basket itself is slightly teardrop/pointed at one end and looks a bit “leaning,” unlike #10 (round tray with spiral base) or #12 (wavy/scalloped rim). Do you see any basket with those colored wraps on the handle?  [15:27:40] director: Great—then please confirm basket 1 too: it’s the mouse/rat-shaped wicker basket in side profile with a pointed snout facing RIGHT, tiny bead eyes, and a flat slot/strap opening on top of its back (not the round cat-face one). [15:27:58] director: Basket 1 check: it’s the SIDE-PROFILE mouse/rat basket—oval body with a pointed snout facing RIGHT and tiny bead eyes; there’s a flat slot/strap opening on top of its back. (Different from basket 6, which is a round cat-face lid seen from above.) Is basket 1 placed?  [15:28:04] director: Perfect—basket 1 is confirmed (mouse/rat side profile with pointed snout to the right). If basket 2 and basket 3 are also set, you should be ready to submit the full 1–12 sequence.  [15:28:15] director: Let’s pin down basket 4 by shape/color: it’s the palest, light cream/straw OVAL basket with ONE thin arch handle, and the whole basket looks like a teardrop (a bit pointier at one end, not a perfect oval). On my image there are small reddish wrap/bindings on the handle near where it meets the basket—do you have ANY very pale teardrop-oval handled basket even if the wraps are hard to see?  [15:39:41] director: Basket 2 is the very DEEP round bucket/half-sphere basket (like a cauldron) with ONE thick, very dark brown arched handle—darker than the basket body. The weave is fine/tight, and there’s a subtle lighter tan band right near the top rim. It’s not shallow like the spiral tray (10) and not the gray-striped squat one (8). Can you confirm you’ve placed that deep round one as position 2?  [15:40:41] director: Basket 3: the rectangular picnic-style wicker basket with a tall upright handle in the center AND a hinged lid that’s open/flipped up to the RIGHT side (you can see the inside). Can you confirm when you’ve placed this as position 3?  [15:41:14] director: Basket 4: the very pale/cream wicker basket with ONE arch handle, but it’s asymmetrical—teardrop shaped with one end pointier/narrower and the opening sits a bit on a diagonal like it’s “leaning.” Look for tiny colored wrap accents (little red/green bands) on the handle near where it meets the sides. Tell me when you’ve placed it.  [15:43:44] director: Basket 5: a shallow, low rectangular basket with straight-ish sides and ONE thick U-shaped handle that looks like twisted/braided rope. The side weave looks chunky and blocky (checker-like). Not the green-striped rectangle (that’s 7) and not the wavy-rim one (12). Confirm when placed.  [15:45:33] director: Basket 6: the round, lidded novelty basket that’s a CAT face seen from above—two little ears, painted eyes/whiskers, and it sits on tiny feet. (Not the mouse/rat side-profile basket 1.) Confirm when you’ve placed it.  [15:46:26] director: Basket 7: the rectangular tote with a high arched handle and a distinct two-tone body—green/teal vertical slats/panels on the sides, with an orange-brown rim and handle plus a braided border along the top. Confirm when you’ve placed basket 7.  [15:47:26] director: Basket 8: a small squat oval basket with ONE arch handle, in gray/brown tones with noticeable horizontal striping/bands (alternating darker and lighter weave). referential_task.1.player.chat_transcrip: [03:09:17] director: Basket 1: a novelty animal basket shaped like a mouse/rat in side profile—rounded oval body with a pointed snout facing right, two tiny bead-like eyes near the nose, and a flat strap/handle opening on top of its back. Let me know when you’ve found it.  [03:09:34] director: Basket 2: a deep, round bucket-style wicker basket with one thick, dark-brown arched handle; the body is very bowl-like/hemispherical and there’s a lighter tan band around the top rim. Confirm when you’ve got it.  [03:10:48] director: Yes—basket 2 has a lighter tan band/stripe running around near the top rim (just below the handle). The handle itself is dark brown.  [03:11:14] director: For basket 2, look for the ONLY one that’s a nearly perfect half-sphere “bucket” with a single high arch handle in very dark wood. The weave is fine and uniform, and there’s a pale strip/band around the upper rim area. It’s not shallow like a tray and not rectangular—more like a rounded cauldron. Do you see any round bowl-shaped basket with a dark handle?  [03:11:49] director: If you’re not seeing the band, prioritize these cues for basket 2: very deep round bowl/hemisphere body + a single thick, very dark arched handle attached at the sides. The body is medium-brown wicker with fine weaving; rim is slightly lighter than the body but the “band” may be subtle. Please place that deep round one with the dark handle as position 2, and tell me when set.  [03:11:59] director: Basket 3: a rectangular picnic-style wicker basket with a tall vertical handle in the center, and its lid is open/hinged up to the RIGHT side (so you can see the interior). Not an open-top basket—has that flap lid. Confirm when placed.  [03:12:10] director: Basket 4: an oval, light-colored basket with a single arched handle; there are colored wrap accents on the handle (little red/green bands), and the basket has a slightly pointed/teardrop shape with the opening angled—looks a bit like it’s “leaning.” Let me know when you’ve got it.  [03:13:15] director: Basket 5: a low, rectangular basket (bread-basket shape) with a single rounded handle; the handle is made of twisted rope-like wicker, and the basket’s sides have a chunky, blocky weave pattern. Confirm when placed.  [03:13:48] director: For basket 5, ignore color and look for structure: it’s the only one that’s a shallow rectangle with straight-ish sides AND a thick U-shaped handle that looks like a twisted braid/rope. The base has a checkerboard/block weave (not vertical slats, not a bucket). Do you have a shallow rectangular “market basket” with a braided handle?  [03:14:19] director: Basket 6: a lidded novelty basket shaped like a cat face viewed from above—round, pale straw, with two small ears, painted eyes/whiskers, and it sits on little feet. It’s more “cat-like” than the mouse in basket 1. Confirm when set.  [03:14:29] director: Basket 7: a rectangular basket with a high arched handle and a two-tone look—greenish/teal vertical slats on the body, with a warm orange-brown rim and handle braid. The top edge has a braided border. Let me know when you find it.  [03:14:39] director: Basket 8: a small, squat oval basket with one handle; overall gray/brown tone with distinct horizontal light-and-dark striping in the weave, and a darker gray handle. It looks “banded”/striped across the body. Confirm when placed.  [03:15:33] director: Let me clarify basket 8: it’s short and wide (not tall), like a little bucket/oval tub with a single arch handle. The weave alternates dark gray-brown and lighter tan bands around the body, giving it a striped look. It’s smaller than the tall cylindrical basket (basket 9) and not rectangular like basket 7. Do you see an oval basket with obvious horizontal striping?  [03:16:00] director: Basket 9: a tall cylindrical wicker basket (like a standing hamper) with two small loop handles near the top rim (one on each side). No big arching handle—just those two little side loops.  [03:16:24] director: Basket 10: a shallow, round tray-like basket with a single arch handle; the base has a noticeable spiral coil pattern, and the handle is light-colored. It’s the flattest, most “plate-like” round one. Confirm when placed.  [03:16:40] director: Basket 11: a large, open-top round basket with two short ear-like handles on the sides (not a tall arch). The weave is dense and diagonally patterned, and the rim flares slightly outward. It looks like a chunky storage bin. Confirm when set.  [03:16:51] director: Basket 12: a medium open-top basket with a single arched handle and a distinctive wavy/scalloped rim opening (dips in the middle). Light brown wicker, more rounded-square than oval, and the handle attaches at the high points of that wavy rim. Confirm when placed.  [15:24:19] director: Great—now basket 2: pick the deep, round “half-sphere” bucket basket with ONE thick, very dark brown arched handle. It’s much deeper than the others (not tray-like), and has a slightly lighter tan band near the top rim. Can you confirm when you’ve placed basket 2?  [15:24:55] director: Basket 7 is the rectangular tote with a high arch handle and a very distinctive two‑tone look: the body has green/teal vertical striping, while the rim and handle are warm orange‑brown with a braided top border. (Not the all-brown shallow one, and not the wavy-rim basket.) Let me know when you’ve placed it.  [15:25:01] director: Basket 3: the rectangular picnic-style wicker basket with a tall vertical handle in the center, and a hinged lid that’s visibly open/flipped up to the RIGHT side (you can see inside). Please confirm when you’ve placed basket 3.  [15:25:42] director: Basket 4: an oval, light-straw basket with a single arched handle that has little colored wrap bands (red/green) near where the handle meets the sides. The opening is slightly asymmetrical/teardrop like it’s leaning. Confirm when you’ve placed basket 4.  [15:27:09] director: Basket 12: open-top light-brown wicker with ONE arched handle, but the key is the rim is wavy/scalloped—there’s a noticeable dip in the middle of the opening (not a straight rectangle). The body is kind of rounded-square. Confirm when you’ve got that one.  [15:27:16] director: Confirmed baskets 4–11. Now double-check basket 1: it’s the mouse/rat-shaped wicker basket in side view with a pointed snout facing right and a flat slot/strap opening on top (not the round cat face). Do you have that placed as position 1?  [15:27:34] director: Let me clarify basket 4: it’s a LIGHT straw-colored OVAL basket with ONE arch handle, but the giveaway is the handle has small COLORED wrap bands (little red/green bits) where the handle meets the sides. The basket itself is slightly teardrop/pointed at one end and looks a bit “leaning,” unlike #10 (round tray with spiral base) or #12 (wavy/scalloped rim). Do you see any basket with those colored wraps on the handle?  [15:27:40] director: Great—then please confirm basket 1 too: it’s the mouse/rat-shaped wicker basket in side profile with a pointed snout facing RIGHT, tiny bead eyes, and a flat slot/strap opening on top of its back (not the round cat-face one). Is that set as position 1?  [15:27:58] director: Basket 1 check: it’s the SIDE-PROFILE mouse/rat basket—oval body with a pointed snout facing RIGHT and tiny bead eyes; there’s a flat slot/strap opening on top of its back. (Different from basket 6, which is a round cat-face lid seen from above.) Is basket 1 placed?  [15:28:04] director: Perfect—basket 1 is confirmed (mouse/rat side profile with pointed snout to the right). If basket 2 and basket 3 are also set, you should be ready to submit the full 1–12 sequence.  [15:28:15] director: Let’s pin down basket 4 by shape/color: it’s the palest, light cream/straw OVAL basket with ONE thin arch handle, and the whole basket looks like a teardrop (a bit pointier at one end, not a perfect oval). It’s not rectangular (like #5/#7), not the shallow round spiral tray (#10), and not the wavy-rim one (#12). On my image there are small reddish wrap/bindings on the handle near where it meets the basket—do you have ANY very pale teardrop-oval handled basket even if the wraps are hard to see?  [15:39:41] director: Basket 2 is the very DEEP round bucket/half-sphere basket (like a cauldron) with ONE thick, very dark brown arched handle—darker than the basket body. The weave is fine/tight, and there’s a subtle lighter tan band right near the top rim. It’s not shallow like the spiral tray (10) and not the gray-striped squat one (8). Can you confirm you’ve placed that deep round one as position 2?  [15:40:41] director: Basket 3: the rectangular picnic-style wicker basket with a tall upright handle in the center AND a hinged lid that’s open/flipped up to the RIGHT side (you can see the inside). Can you confirm when you’ve placed this as position 3?  [15:41:14] director: Basket 4: the very pale/cream wicker basket with ONE arch handle, but it’s asymmetrical—teardrop shaped with one end pointier/narrower and the opening sits a bit on a diagonal like it’s “leaning.” Look for tiny colored wrap accents (little red/green bands) on the handle near where it meets the sides. Tell me when you’ve placed it.  [15:43:44] director: Basket 5: a shallow, low rectangular basket with straight-ish sides and ONE thick U-shaped handle that looks like twisted/braided rope. The side weave looks chunky and blocky (checker-like). Not the green-striped rectangle (that’s 7) and not the wavy-rim one (12). Confirm when placed.  [15:45:33] director: Basket 6: the round, lidded novelty basket that’s a CAT face seen from above—two little ears, painted eyes/whiskers, and it sits on tiny feet. (Not the mouse/rat side-profile basket 1.) Confirm when you’ve placed it.  [15:46:26] director: Basket 7: the rectangular tote with a high arched handle and a distinct two-tone body—green/teal vertical slats/panels on the sides, with an orange-brown rim and handle plus a braided border along the top. Confirm when you’ve placed basket 7.  [15:47:26] director: Basket 8: a small squat oval basket with ONE arch handle, in gray/brown tones with noticeable horizontal striping/bands (alternating darker and lighter weave). The handle is darker gray than the body. Confirm when placed.

[03:14:29] director: Basket 7: a rectangular basket with a high arched handle and a two-tone look—greenish/teal vertical slats on the body, with a warm orange-brown rim and handle braid. referential_task.1.player.chat_transcrip: [03:09:17] director: Basket 1: a novelty animal basket shaped like a mouse/rat in side profile—rounded oval body with a pointed snout facing right, two tiny bead-like eyes near the nose, and a flat strap/handle opening on top of its back. Let me know when you’ve found it.  [03:09:34] director: Basket 2: a deep, round bucket-style wicker basket with one thick, dark-brown arched handle; the body is very bowl-like/hemispherical and there’s a lighter tan band around the top rim. Confirm when you’ve got it.  [03:10:48] director: Yes—basket 2 has a lighter tan band/stripe running around near the top rim (just below the handle). The handle itself is dark brown.  [03:11:14] director: For basket 2, look for the ONLY one that’s a nearly perfect half-sphere “bucket” with a single high arch handle in very dark wood. The weave is fine and uniform, and there’s a pale strip/band around the upper rim area. It’s not shallow like a tray and not rectangular—more like a rounded cauldron. Do you see any round bowl-shaped basket with a dark handle?  [03:11:49] director: If you’re not seeing the band, prioritize these cues for basket 2: very deep round bowl/hemisphere body + a single thick, very dark arched handle attached at the sides. The body is medium-brown wicker with fine weaving; rim is slightly lighter than the body but the “band” may be subtle. Please place that deep round one with the dark handle as position 2, and tell me when set.  [03:11:59] director: Basket 3: a rectangular picnic-style wicker basket with a tall vertical handle in the center, and its lid is open/hinged up to the RIGHT side (so you can see the interior). Not an open-top basket—has that flap lid. Confirm when placed.  [03:12:10] director: Basket 4: an oval, light-colored basket with a single arched handle; there are colored wrap accents on the handle (little red/green bands), and the basket has a slightly pointed/teardrop shape with the opening angled—looks a bit like it’s “leaning.” Let me know when you’ve got it.  [03:13:15] director: Basket 5: a low, rectangular basket (bread-basket shape) with a single rounded handle; the handle is made of twisted rope-like wicker, and the basket’s sides have a chunky, blocky weave pattern. Confirm when placed.  [03:13:48] director: For basket 5, ignore color and look for structure: it’s the only one that’s a shallow rectangle with straight-ish sides AND a thick U-shaped handle that looks like a twisted braid/rope. The base has a checkerboard/block weave (not vertical slats, not a bucket). Do you have a shallow rectangular “market basket” with a braided handle?  [03:14:19] director: Basket 6: a lidded novelty basket shaped like a cat face viewed from above—round, pale straw, with two small ears, painted eyes/whiskers, and it sits on little feet. It’s more “cat-like” than the mouse in basket 1. Confirm when set.  [03:14:29] director: Basket 7: a rectangular basket with a high arched handle and a two-tone look—greenish/teal vertical slats on the body, with a warm orange-brown rim and handle braid. The top edge has a braided border. Let me know when you find it.  [03:14:39] director: Basket 8: a small, squat oval basket with one handle; overall gray/brown tone with distinct horizontal light-and-dark striping in the weave, and a darker gray handle. It looks “banded”/striped across the body. Confirm when placed.  [03:15:33] director: Let me clarify basket 8: it’s short and wide (not tall), like a little bucket/oval tub with a single arch handle. The weave alternates dark gray-brown and lighter tan bands around the body, giving it a striped look. It’s smaller than the tall cylindrical basket (basket 9) and not rectangular like basket 7. Do you see an oval basket with obvious horizontal striping?  [03:16:00] director: Basket 9: a tall cylindrical wicker basket (like a standing hamper) with two small loop handles near the top rim (one on each side). No big arching handle—just those two little side loops.  [03:16:24] director: Basket 10: a shallow, round tray-like basket with a single arch handle; the base has a noticeable spiral coil pattern, and the handle is light-colored. It’s the flattest, most “plate-like” round one. Confirm when placed.  [03:16:40] director: Basket 11: a large, open-top round basket with two short ear-like handles on the sides (not a tall arch). The weave is dense and diagonally patterned, and the rim flares slightly outward. It looks like a chunky storage bin. Confirm when set.  [03:16:51] director: Basket 12: a medium open-top basket with a single arched handle and a distinctive wavy/scalloped rim opening (dips in the middle). Light brown wicker, more rounded-square than oval, and the handle attaches at the high points of that wavy rim. Confirm when placed.  [15:24:19] director: Great—now basket 2: pick the deep, round “half-sphere” bucket basket with ONE thick, very dark brown arched handle. It’s much deeper than the others (not tray-like), and has a slightly lighter tan band near the top rim. Can you confirm when you’ve placed basket 2?  [15:24:55] director: Basket 7 is the rectangular tote with a high arch handle and a very distinctive two‑tone look: the body has green/teal vertical striping, while the rim and handle are warm orange‑brown with a braided top border. (Not the all-brown shallow one, and not the wavy-rim basket.) Let me know when you’ve placed it.  [15:25:01] director: Basket 3: the rectangular picnic-style wicker basket with a tall vertical handle in the center, and a hinged lid that’s visibly open/flipped up to the RIGHT side (you can see inside). Please confirm when you’ve placed basket 3.  [15:25:42] director: Basket 4: an oval, light-straw basket with a single arched handle that has little colored wrap bands (red/green) near where the handle meets the sides. The opening is slightly asymmetrical/teardrop like it’s leaning. Confirm when you’ve placed basket 4.  [15:27:09] director: Basket 12: open-top light-brown wicker with ONE arched handle, but the key is the rim is wavy/scalloped—there’s a noticeable dip in the middle of the opening (not a straight rectangle). The body is kind of rounded-square. Confirm when you’ve got that one.  [15:27:16] director: Confirmed baskets 4–11. Now double-check basket 1: it’s the mouse/rat-shaped wicker basket in side view with a pointed snout facing right and a flat slot/strap opening on top (not the round cat face). Do you have that placed as position 1?  [15:27:34] director: Let me clarify basket 4: it’s a LIGHT straw-colored OVAL basket with ONE arch handle, but the giveaway is the handle has small COLORED wrap bands (little red/green bits) where the handle meets the sides. The basket itself is slightly teardrop/pointed at one end and looks a bit “leaning,” unlike #10 (round tray with spiral base) or #12 (wavy/scalloped rim). Do you see any basket with those colored wraps on the handle?  [15:27:40] director: Great—then please confirm basket 1 too: it’s the mouse/rat-shaped wicker basket in side profile with a pointed snout facing RIGHT, tiny bead eyes, and a flat slot/strap opening on top of its back (not the round cat-face one). Is that set as position 1?  [15:27:58] director: Basket 1 check: it’s the SIDE-PROFILE mouse/rat basket—oval body with a pointed snout facing RIGHT and tiny bead eyes; there’s a flat slot/strap opening on top of its back. (Different from basket 6, which is a round cat-face lid seen from above.) Is basket 1 placed?  [15:28:04] director: Perfect—basket 1 is confirmed (mouse/rat side profile with pointed snout to the right). If basket 2 and basket 3 are also set, you should be ready to submit the full 1–12 sequence.  [15:28:15] director: Let’s pin down basket 4 by shape/color: it’s the palest, light cream/straw OVAL basket with ONE thin arch handle, and the whole basket looks like a teardrop (a bit pointier at one end, not a perfect oval). It’s not rectangular (like #5/#7), not the shallow round spiral tray (#10), and not the wavy-rim one (#12). On my image there are small reddish wrap/bindings on the handle near where it meets the basket—do you have ANY very pale teardrop-oval handled basket even if the wraps are hard to see?  [15:39:41] director: Basket 2 is the very DEEP round bucket/half-sphere basket (like a cauldron) with ONE thick, very dark brown arched handle—darker than the basket body. The weave is fine/tight, and there’s a subtle lighter tan band right near the top rim. It’s not shallow like the spiral tray (10) and not the gray-striped squat one (8). Can you confirm you’ve placed that deep round one as position 2?  [15:40:41] director: Basket 3: the rectangular picnic-style wicker basket with a tall upright handle in the center AND a hinged lid that’s open/flipped up to the RIGHT side (you can see the inside). Can you confirm when you’ve placed this as position 3?  [15:41:14] director: Basket 4: the very pale/cream wicker basket with ONE arch handle, but it’s asymmetrical—teardrop shaped with one end pointier/narrower and the opening sits a bit on a diagonal like it’s “leaning.” Look for tiny colored wrap accents (little red/green bands) on the handle near where it meets the sides. Tell me when you’ve placed it.  [15:43:44] director: Basket 5: a shallow, low rectangular basket with straight-ish sides and ONE thick U-shaped handle that looks like twisted/braided rope. The side weave looks chunky and blocky (checker-like). Not the green-striped rectangle (that’s 7) and not the wavy-rim one (12). Confirm when placed.  [15:45:33] director: Basket 6: the round, lidded novelty basket that’s a CAT face seen from above—two little ears, painted eyes/whiskers, and it sits on tiny feet. (Not the mouse/rat side-profile basket 1.) Confirm when you’ve placed it.  [15:46:26] director: Basket 7: the rectangular tote with a high arched handle and a distinct two-tone body—green/teal vertical slats/panels on the sides, with an orange-brown rim and handle plus a braided border along the top. Confirm when you’ve placed basket 7.  [15:47:26] director: Basket 8: a small squat oval basket with ONE arch handle, in gray/brown tones with noticeable horizontal striping/bands (alternating darker and lighter weave). The handle is darker gray than the body. Confirm when placed.
